title: Whole Genome Sequencing of Human Bacterial Pathogens Protocol
author: Sanjay Gautam
license_note: Confirmed via CrossRef DOI metadata: dx.doi.org/10.17504/protocols.io.4xxgxpn
materials: High Pure PCR Template Preparation Kit

steps.title: Pellet the liquid culture media (...
steps.instruction: Pellet the liquid culture media ( 200 µL ) by centrifuging at for 00:08:00 in a sterile microfuge tube. CRITICAL STEP: All bacterial cultures should be treated as potentially pathogenic to the laboratory worker and colleagues. Therefore, the use of appropriate aseptic techniques, and the wearing of appropriate personal protective equipment are strongly recommended to maintain acceptable work health and safety standards and minimise exposure to harmful agents.

steps.title: Resuspend the pellet in 600 µL...
steps.instruction: Resuspend the pellet in 600 µL phosphate-buffered saline (1x) until the absorbance at 600 nm (A 600 ) is between 1.0 and 2.0.

steps.title: Lyse the cells by adding 30...
steps.instruction: Lyse the cells by adding 30 µL lysozyme (50 mg/ml), vortex, and incubate at 37 °C for 01:00:00 .

steps.title: Follow the DNeasy® Blood and Tissue...
steps.instruction: Follow the DNeasy® Blood and Tissue Kit Quick-start protocol to extract the DNA.

steps.title: Elute the DNA in 100 µL volume
steps.instruction: Elute the DNA in 100 µL volume.

steps.title: Treat it with 2 µL RNase...
steps.instruction: Treat it with 2 µL RNase (100 mg/ml) (Qiagen, Hilden, Germany) and incubate at Room temperature for 01:00:00 .

steps.title: Purify RNase-treated DNA using the High...
steps.instruction: Purify RNase-treated DNA using the High Pure PCR Template Preparation Kit . TIP: Perform only 4 DNA spin-wash steps instead of 9 recommended steps. Pre-incubate the elution buffer in a heat block set at 70 °C .

steps.title: Add 100 µL of binding buffer...
steps.instruction: Add 100 µL of binding buffer to RNase treated DNA and incubate at 70 °C for 00:10:00 .

steps.title: Add 50 µL of 2-Propanol and...
steps.instruction: Add 50 µL of 2-Propanol and transfer the content to a Roche spin column and spin at for 00:01:00 .

steps.title: Discard the flow through and insert...
steps.instruction: Discard the flow through and insert the spin column into a new collection tube.

steps.title: Wash by adding 500 µL wash...
steps.instruction: Wash by adding 500 µL wash buffer and spin at for 00:01:00 .

steps.title: Discard the flow through and insert...
steps.instruction: Discard the flow through and insert the spin column into a new collection tube.

steps.title: Perform a final spin at for 00:01:00
steps.instruction: Perform a final spin at for 00:01:00 .

steps.title: Finally, insert the column into a 1
steps.instruction: Finally, insert the column into a 1.5 ml sterile microfuge tube, add 50 µL of pre-heated elution buffer and spin at for 00:01:00 to elute the purified DNA for next generation sequencing. CRITICAL STEP: For next generation sequencing, contaminant-free, high-molecular weight DNA with an absorbance (260 nm/280 nm) ratio between 1.8 to 2.0 is considered a high-quality template DNA.

steps.title: Dispense 190 µL and 198 µL...
steps.instruction: Dispense 190 µL and 198 µL of Qubit working solution in standard and sample tubes, respectively.

steps.title: Add 10 µL standards (1 and...
steps.instruction: Add 10 µL standards (1 and 2) and 2 µL of sample in separate Qubit assay tubes.

steps.title: Vortex the mixture for 00:00:03 and...
steps.instruction: Vortex the mixture for 00:00:03 and incubate at Room temperature for 00:02:00 before taking the reading.

steps.title: Adjust the DNA concentration of each sample to 0
steps.instruction: Adjust the DNA concentration of each sample to 0.2 ng/μl by diluting with a required volume of distilled water. CRITICAL STEP: The use of an accurate concentration of DNA is crucial for bacterial DNA genomic library preparation.

steps.title: TIP
steps.instruction: TIP: For all of the methods below, the recommended 96-well TYC plate can be replaced with 0.2 ml thin wall clear, flat capped PCR tubes. In addition, multichannel pipettes and the high-speed micro plate shaker can be replaced with single channel pipettes and a bench top centrifuge, respectively.

steps.title: In a PCR tube, add 5...
steps.instruction: In a PCR tube, add 5 µL tagmentation DNA buffer and 2.5 µL amplification tagmentation mix to 2.5 µL (0.2 ng/μl) input DNA.

steps.title: Briefly vortexed the content and transfer...
steps.instruction: Briefly vortexed the content and transfer to the thermocycler programmed for one step at 55 °C for 00:05:00 with heated lid, followed by a hold at 10 °C for a volume of 10 µL .

steps.title: Immediately after reaching the hold temperature...
steps.instruction: Immediately after reaching the hold temperature of 10 °C in the above step, neutralize NTA by adding 2.5 µL neutralization tagmentation buffer and incubate at Room temperature for 00:05:00 .

steps.title: For amplification, add 7
steps.instruction: For amplification, add 7.5 µL Nextera® PCR mastermix and 2.5 µL of each index primer, 1 and 2, to a tube containing neutralized NTA. CRITICAL STEP: Primer combinations, S502 with N705 /706 and S503 with N701/702 should be avoided. Avoid any repeated combinations and carefully note the primers used for each sample.

steps.title: Gently pipette the content and perform a quick spin
steps.instruction: Gently pipette the content and perform a quick spin.

steps.title: Proceed to amplification in a thermocycler...
steps.instruction: Proceed to amplification in a thermocycler programmed for a working volume of 25 µL with the following settings and a heated lid: %s %s The amplified, tagmented library can be stored at 4 °C overnight for PCR clean-up the next day.

steps.title: NOTE
steps.instruction: NOTE: Bring AMPure XP beads to room temperature (for 00:20:00 ). CRITICAL STEP: Prepare fresh 80% (v/v) ethanol and 0.2 M NaOH.

steps.title: To 22
steps.instruction: To 22.5 µL of PCR product, add 11.25 µL of vortexed ( 00:00:30 ) AMPure XP beads and mix by pipetting (10 times).

steps.title: Incubate at room temperature for 00:05:00
steps.instruction: Incubate at room temperature for 00:05:00 .

steps.title: Place the tube on a magnetic stand for 00:02:00
steps.instruction: Place the tube on a magnetic stand for 00:02:00 .

steps.title: While leaving the PCR tubes on...
steps.instruction: While leaving the PCR tubes on the magnetic stand, carefully aspirate the supernatant. CRITICAL STEP: Do not aspirate beads. If aspirated, redo steps 24 and 25.

steps.title: Add 100 µL of 80% ethanol...
steps.instruction: Add 100 µL of 80% ethanol and leave on the stand in the magnetic stand for 00:00:30 . CRITICAL STEP: Do not resuspend the beads.

steps.title: Aspirate out the supernatant carefully
steps.instruction: Aspirate out the supernatant carefully.

steps.title: Add 100 µL of 80% ethanol...
steps.instruction: Add 100 µL of 80% ethanol and leave on the stand in the magnetic stand for 00:00:30 .

steps.title: Aspirate out the supernatant carefully
steps.instruction: Aspirate out the supernatant carefully.

steps.title: Remove the tube from magnetic stand...
steps.instruction: Remove the tube from magnetic stand and allow to air dry in a tube stand for approximately 00:05:00 . CRITICAL STEP: Visually check for cracks as over drying the beads will significantly reduce elution efficiency.

steps.title: Add 26
steps.instruction: Add 26.15 µL of resuspension buffer and gently pipette 20 times to mix.

steps.title: Incubate the tubes at room temperature...
steps.instruction: Incubate the tubes at room temperature for 00:02:00 and then place on a magnetic stand for 00:02:00 (until the supernatant cleared).

steps.title: Transfer the supernatant ( 25 µL...
steps.instruction: Transfer the supernatant ( 25 µL ) to a new PCR tube. NOTE: The final supernatant can be stored at − 15°C to − 20°C for up to 1 week but we recommend proceeding to library normalization immediately.

steps.title: Perform the Qubit DNA quantification method...
steps.instruction: Perform the Qubit DNA quantification method as described above to determine the genomic DNA concentration in cleaned up product.

steps.title: Pool the genomic DNA from all of the tubes. NOTE
steps.instruction: Pool the genomic DNA from all of the tubes. NOTE: Sample with the lowest DNA concentration can be used in a volume of 10 µL as the reference to prepare a library pool using the formula: Volume required (V2) = Concentration original (S1) x Volume total (V1 = 10 μl) / Concentration required (S2).

steps.title: To x μl of library pool,...
steps.instruction: To x μl of library pool, add x μl of freshly prepared 0.2 molar NaOH (final concentration 0.1 molar) and incubate for 00:05:00 at Room temperature .

steps.title: To the NaOH treated suspension add...
steps.instruction: To the NaOH treated suspension add an equal volume (2 x μl) of LNS1. Label the tube as pooled amplified library (PAL). TIPS: In this modified step, normalize the library by using LNS1 (Library Normalisation Storage Buffer 1) only.

steps.title: Dilute the PAL to 1:1000 by...
steps.instruction: Dilute the PAL to 1:1000 by adding 1 µL of NaOH-LNS1 treated suspension to 999 µL of ultrapure distilled water.

steps.title: Use KAPA library quantification kit (No...
steps.instruction: Use KAPA library quantification kit (No ROX) to check the concentration of diluted pooled library in a real time PCR system using the following set up: %s %s NOTE: Include a set of six DNA standards (with concentrations ranging from 20 pM to 0.0002 pM), three sets of negative control (ultrapure distilled water), and three sets of the DNA library in the qPCR run.

steps.title: Determine the concentration of DNA in...
steps.instruction: Determine the concentration of DNA in the pooled library by the standard curve method and calculate concentration in picomolar (pM) for each tube. NOTE: To calculate the original concentration of the pooled library we applied the formula: [Average sample concentration (in pM) . insert size standards (452 bp) . dilution factor (1000)] divisible by the Insert size of pooled library (500 BP) For example, for a qPCR determined concentration of 2.36 pM in a 1:1000 dilution of the pooled library, the library DNA concentration will be: (2.36 pM x 452 bp x 1000) / 500 bp = 2133.44 pM The value obtained from the calculation represents the concentration of DNA in the pooled library.

steps.title: To estimate the dilution factor required...
steps.instruction: To estimate the dilution factor required to achieve a final library concentration of 15 picomolar (pM) in a 600 µL volume use the formula: Volume required = (Concentration required × Volume total ) / Concentration original = (15 pM × 600 μl) / 2133.44 pM = 4.22 μl NOTE: Therefore, 4.22 µL is added to 595.78 µL of HT buffer to produce a final concentration of 15 picomolar (pM) , in a final volume of 600 µL . The diluted library is then ready to be heat denatured and loaded into the MiSeq reagent cartridge.

steps.title: Thaw the PAL at room temperature...
steps.instruction: Thaw the PAL at room temperature and mix by pipetting up and down (5 times) followed by brief centrifugation.

steps.title: Based on the library concentration example above, transfer 595
steps.instruction: Based on the library concentration example above, transfer 595.78 µL of HT buffer to a 1.5 mL diluted amplified library (DAL) tube containing 4.22 µL PAL.

steps.title: Mix using a pipette (5 times)
steps.instruction: Mix using a pipette (5 times).

steps.title: Vortex the DAL tube at top...
steps.instruction: Vortex the DAL tube at top speed, centrifuge briefly , and incubate exactly for 00:02:00 at 96 °C ± 2 °C .

steps.title: Immediately transfer the DAL tube to...
steps.instruction: Immediately transfer the DAL tube to ice for at least 00:05:00 or until loading. CRITICAL STEP: Put the Illumina MiSeq sequencer through a short wash cycle to avoid cross-contamination of the DAL from previous usage.

steps.title: Thaw the MiSeq reagent cartridge at Room temperature
steps.instruction: Thaw the MiSeq reagent cartridge at Room temperature .

steps.title: Generate a MiSeq sample sheet using the Illumina Experiment Manager
steps.instruction: Generate a MiSeq sample sheet using the Illumina Experiment Manager. See step 22 ( go to step #22 ) to identify primer sets for each sample.

steps.title: Use the following configuration to set up the Miseq machine
steps.instruction: Use the following configuration to set up the Miseq machine. Generate FASTQ workflow; FASTQ Only application; NexteraXT assay; 151 insert reads; assignment of the samples with a unique identifier and index-pair combination. CRITICAL STEP: Rinse the flow cell with MilliQ water and remove traces of water using soft tissue paper before inserting into the machine.

steps.title: Transfer the entire 600 µL of...
steps.instruction: Transfer the entire 600 µL of DAL to the “Load here” well of the MiSeq reagent cartridge.

steps.title: Following the setup procedure of the...
steps.instruction: Following the setup procedure of the Illumina Experiment Manager, insert the cartridge into MiSeq instrument for sequencing to commence. TIPS: The raw FastQ sequence reads from whole-genome sequencing can be stored on the local computer as well as on the Illumina BaseSpace server (https://basespace.illumina.com/) for further analysis.

steps.title: NOTE
steps.instruction: NOTE: The selection of bioinformatics software for the analysis of WGS data will be determined by the objective of the study. Here, we used Geneious 9.1.8 (Biomatters Ltd.), a desktop software to analyse our sequence data. Geneious was used to map the Fastq sequence reads to a publicly available reference genome for each species as follows:

steps.title: Download Geneious
steps.instruction: Download Geneious .

steps.title: Go to File | Import | From File
steps.instruction: Go to File | Import | From File. Import raw-read files (Sample_xx_R1.fastq.gz and Sample_xx_R2.fastq. gz) into Geneious.

steps.title: Download the Reference Genome from the NCBI database
steps.instruction: Download the Reference Genome from the NCBI database. For example, Enterococcus faecium NC_017960. In the Left panel | Go to NCBI | Nucleotide.

steps.title: Enter NC_017960 | Click Search
steps.instruction: Enter NC_017960 | Click Search.

steps.title: Once the genome has been found, click Download Full Sequence(s)
steps.instruction: Once the genome has been found, click Download Full Sequence(s) .

steps.title: Download the NC_017960 reference genome (The...
steps.instruction: Download the NC_017960 reference genome (The icon changes to a green circular genome when completed).

steps.title: Drag and drop the NC_017960 reference...
steps.instruction: Drag and drop the NC_017960 reference genome into the working folder.

steps.title: Mapping the isolate sequence to the...
steps.instruction: Mapping the isolate sequence to the reference genome Hold CTRL and select both R1 and R2 raw read files (imported), and the reference genome (NC_017960) (downloaded).

steps.title: Click Align | Assemble|Map to Reference
steps.instruction: Click Align | Assemble|Map to Reference.

steps.title: Check the settings
steps.instruction: Check the settings

steps.title: Reference Sequence = NC_017960
steps.instruction: Reference Sequence = NC_017960

steps.title: Mapper = Bowtie2–fast and accurate read mapper
steps.instruction: Mapper = Bowtie2–fast and accurate read mapper

steps.title: Trim Before Mapping = Do not trim
steps.instruction: Trim Before Mapping = Do not trim

steps.title: Results
steps.instruction: Results: Select all options

steps.title: Results | Save consensus sequences | Options
steps.instruction: Results | Save consensus sequences | Options

steps.title: Threshold = Highest Quality
steps.instruction: Threshold = Highest Quality

steps.title: Threshold for sequences without quality = 95%
steps.instruction: Threshold for sequences without quality = 95%

steps.title: No coverage call = ‘–’
steps.instruction: No coverage call = ‘–’

steps.title: When mapping to reference is complete,...
steps.instruction: When mapping to reference is complete, a new folder will be created containing four files:

steps.title: Assembly Report
steps.instruction: Assembly Report

steps.title: Consensus
steps.instruction: Consensus

steps.title: Contig
steps.instruction: Contig

steps.title: Unused Reads
steps.instruction: Unused Reads

steps.title: NOTE
steps.instruction: NOTE: Setting may vary depending on objective of analyses and quality of fastq reads. NOTE: We also used open source databases, for example, TGS-TB, PhyResSe and the Center for Genomic Epidemiology’s [20] ResFinder and VirulenceFinde, to further analyse the whole genome sequence data of our selection of bacterial pathogens. These freely-available databases enable the acquisition of information on bacterial pathogens that included genotype and phylogeny, antibiotic-resistance mutations, and the presence of known virulence genes.
